element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Players learn spatial orientation via overworld path system before combat encounters
North-to-east path bends and branching routes teach map reading without threat
Pamela Voorhees' cave represents climactic dangerous space; candle elements suggest environmental hazards

rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Oscillates between safe overworld map phases and dangerous interior/cave combat encounters; tension concentrated in environmental hazards and boss confrontations

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Path branching on camp map; hidden path details suggest optional exploration routes (sprite-based path alternatives, palette-hidden sections)
spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Green shading on paths may indicate special or alternate routes; palette-hidden tiles suggest concealed connections

difficulty_curve.early_game: Low-threat camp exploration establishes spatial familiarity
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Introduction of interior hazards and confined combat spaces
difficulty_curve.late_game: Pamela Voorhees cave confrontation with environmental obstacles (candle hazards)
notable_spikes: Transition from safe camp map to first dangerous interior location
Pamela Voorhees boss encounter in cave environment

notable_level_patterns.description: Game architecture alternates between safe overworld camp map (with clear path navigation and palette-based wayfinding) and dangerous interior/cave spaces with combat and environmental hazards

Overworld uses sprite-based path shading and background path graphics with matching green tones; north-to-east bends create natural directional flow; unused palette variants suggest cut branching paths
Candle elements in cave suggest fire/light-based hazards increase danger in late-game confined spaces; smaller candle variants indicate scalable threat levels

sources_used: tcrf (The Cutting Room Floor) - Friday the 13th NES article: camp map details, palette system, path graphics, cave environment, candles, unused items
